title: Understanding Transformers from first principles
description: A clear-eyed look at the mechanics, the trade-offs, and the parts most write-ups quietly skip over.
author: Dr. Lena Park

quickChecks.q: In scaled dot-product attention, the query–key dot product is divided by what before the softmax?
options: The square root of the key dimension
quickChecks.why: Dividing by √dₖ keeps the dot products from growing large in magnitude, which would otherwise push softmax into a near one-hot regime with vanishing gradients.

quickChecks.q: Offline eval accuracy sits far above live production performance. The most likely culprit is…
options: The learning rate is too low
quickChecks.why: A gap that large almost always means the eval set leaked into training, or it no longer matches the live distribution — rarely a raw capacity problem.

quiz.q: What does retrieval-augmented generation primarily reduce?
Quantizing weights to int8 mainly trades…
options: Memory and latency for a small accuracy hit

quiz.q: A mixture-of-experts layer grows total parameters while keeping what roughly constant?
The √dₖ scaling inside attention exists to…
